## Summary

Removes all internal pipeline terminology from the evaluation progress page. Progress bar labels now use author-friendly language that reveals nothing about how the system works internally. The redundant milestone list below the progress bar is deleted entirely — the progress bar alone is sufficient.

**Before:** "Ingesting manuscript & mapping chapters...", "Calibrating evaluation standards — complete (12s · 749 words)", "Manuscript analysis + structural preflight — in progress...", "Running deep structural craft diagnostics...", "Assembling evaluation matrix...", "Running final structural cross-checks...", "Waiting in queue"

**After:** "Reading your manuscript...", "Understanding your story...", "Analyzing your manuscript in depth...", "Finalizing your evaluation...", "Putting the finishing touches on your report...", "Starting your evaluation...", "Getting ready..."

Also fixes the "Waiting in queue" badge — now always shows "In progress" for any non-complete/non-failed job. No more stale SSR badge.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `components/evaluation-poller-display.ts` — all progress labels + helper texts replaced
- `components/EvaluationPoller.tsx` — pipeline stage mini-timeline deleted
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — SSR badge simplified (no more "Waiting in queue")
- `tests/evaluation-poller-display.test.ts` — all label assertions updated, jargon blocklist expanded

**Does NOT touch:**
- No evaluation pipeline changes, no prompt changes, no scoring changes
- No API route changes, no database changes

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- The milestone list provided a phase-by-phase breakdown that some power users might have liked. However, it leaked IP and was redundant with the progress bar label.
- No functional risk — this is purely a display/copy change.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no evaluation pipeline or prompt changes. UI copy and display only.
